text_fact_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark).

text_fact_location_accuracy.matching_rule: unit_index of the matched element == expected_location.unit_index (only facts declaring one)

unexpected_text_duplication.matching_rule: count of canonical text elements whose normalized text exactly repeats an already-matched expected fact

identifier_unique_recall.matching_rule: IDENTIFIER_BOUNDARY_V1: exact, case-SENSITIVE substring match where the character immediately before and after the identifier is not alphanumeric -- regex (?<![A-Za-z0-9])<identifier>(?![A-Za-z0-9]), applied to raw (unnormalized) text. Case-sensitive deliberately: identifiers in this manifest are always uppercase and case-folding risks merging distinct-looking tokens.

identifier_occurrence_recall.matching_rule: IDENTIFIER_BOUNDARY_V1: exact, case-SENSITIVE substring match where the character immediately before and after the identifier is not alphanumeric -- regex (?<![A-Za-z0-9])<identifier>(?![A-Za-z0-9]), applied to raw (unnormalized) text. Case-sensitive deliberately: identifiers in this manifest are always uppercase and case-folding risks merging distinct-looking tokens.

identifier_distractor_no_false_merge.matching_rule: IDENTIFIER_BOUNDARY_V1: exact, case-SENSITIVE substring match where the character immediately before and after the identifier is not alphanumeric -- regex (?<![A-Za-z0-9])<identifier>(?![A-Za-z0-9]), applied to raw (unnormalized) text. Case-sensitive deliberately: identifiers in this manifest are always uppercase and case-folding risks merging distinct-looking tokens. -- verifies a distractor identifier's occurrences are never counted toward a target identifier's occurrence tally

heading_text_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark).

heading_level_accuracy.matching_rule: of headings correctly classified as CanonicalHeading, exact level match

heading_classification_accuracy.matching_rule: of headings whose text matched, fraction represented as CanonicalHeading (not degraded to CanonicalParagraph)

table_presence.matching_rule: cell-text-overlap table identification (>= half of expected cell texts present)

table_structure_accuracy.matching_rule: exact n_rows/n_cols match, of tables identified

table_cell_text_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark).

table_cell_coordinate_accuracy.matching_rule: exact (row, col) match, of cells whose text was found

table_header_status_accuracy.matching_rule: is_header match, of cells whose text was found

table_span_accuracy.matching_rule: row_span/col_span exact match, of cells whose text was found

picture_presence.matching_rule: at least one CanonicalPicture present per expected picture fact

caption_text_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark). -- matched against CanonicalCaption OR CanonicalParagraph text (text recovery is scored independently of caption-picture linkage)

caption_linkage_accuracy.matching_rule: of captions whose text was found (as caption or paragraph), represented as a real CanonicalCaption with target_picture_id resolving to the expected picture

ocr_token_recall.matching_rule: case-folded substring match (either direction) against OcrAnnotation.text -- OCR engines may merge/split tokens

provenance_bbox_coverage_heading.matching_rule: has a ProvenanceEntry AND that entry's bbox is not None (bbox absence is not treated as total provenance absence)

provenance_bbox_coverage_paragraph.matching_rule: has a ProvenanceEntry AND that entry's bbox is not None (bbox absence is not treated as total provenance absence)

expected_value.text: Application APP-224510 supports the Payment Settlement business service.
APP-224510 is in scope for Regulatory Obligation O-31.
Regulatory Obligation O-31 is satisfied by Control C-88.
Control C-88 mandates Recovery Procedure P-205 for critical payment-processing applications.
Payment Settlement is owned by the Resilience Engineering Team and operates in EMEA.
APP-224499 was the predecessor system for payment settlement and was decommissioned in 2024; it is superseded by APP-224510.
Legacy Control C-88a was retired and replaced by Control C-88; references to C-88a in older documents are historical only.
Enterprise Resilience Overview: Payment Settlement Service
Recovery Objectives
Escalation Flow
Figure 1: Escalation flow from incident detection to activation of Recovery Procedure P-205.
Incident Detected
Escalate to Resilience Team
Activate Procedure P-205

miss_records.explanation: chart_visual_stress declares visual_facts (CF_*/CC_*/CU_*, numeric-value facts requiring a VisionEnricher path A does not have) but no expected_ocr_tokens/expected_ocr_text field -- unlike the parity picture and scanned-PDF fixtures, this manifest section provides no frozen ground truth an evaluator could score raw chart-label OCR recall against without inventing expected values. Proposed fix: a separate, versioned evaluation-profile addendum (never a frozen-manifest edit) adding an explicit expected_ocr_tokens list for chart_visual_stress.

expected_value.text: This page intentionally contains no digital text layer. Recovery Procedure P-205 must be tested quarterly.

text_fact_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark).

text_fact_location_accuracy.matching_rule: unit_index of the matched element == expected_location.unit_index (only facts declaring one)

unexpected_text_duplication.matching_rule: count of canonical text elements whose normalized text exactly repeats an already-matched expected fact

identifier_unique_recall.matching_rule: IDENTIFIER_BOUNDARY_V1: exact, case-SENSITIVE substring match where the character immediately before and after the identifier is not alphanumeric -- regex (?<![A-Za-z0-9])<identifier>(?![A-Za-z0-9]), applied to raw (unnormalized) text. Case-sensitive deliberately: identifiers in this manifest are always uppercase and case-folding risks merging distinct-looking tokens.

identifier_occurrence_recall.matching_rule: IDENTIFIER_BOUNDARY_V1: exact, case-SENSITIVE substring match where the character immediately before and after the identifier is not alphanumeric -- regex (?<![A-Za-z0-9])<identifier>(?![A-Za-z0-9]), applied to raw (unnormalized) text. Case-sensitive deliberately: identifiers in this manifest are always uppercase and case-folding risks merging distinct-looking tokens.

identifier_distractor_no_false_merge.matching_rule: IDENTIFIER_BOUNDARY_V1: exact, case-SENSITIVE substring match where the character immediately before and after the identifier is not alphanumeric -- regex (?<![A-Za-z0-9])<identifier>(?![A-Za-z0-9]), applied to raw (unnormalized) text. Case-sensitive deliberately: identifiers in this manifest are always uppercase and case-folding risks merging distinct-looking tokens. -- verifies a distractor identifier's occurrences are never counted toward a target identifier's occurrence tally

heading_classification_accuracy.matching_rule: of headings whose text matched, fraction represented as CanonicalHeading (not degraded to CanonicalParagraph)

table_structure_accuracy.matching_rule: exact n_rows/n_cols match, of tables identified

diagram_label_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark).

no_invented_diagram_relationships.matching_rule: Stage 5A must never produce DiagramNode/EdgeAnnotation (no VisionEnricher exists) -- verifies zero such annotations exist

diagram_edge_recovery.matching_rule: DiagramEdgeAnnotation recovery -- structurally not_applicable to path A (no VisionEnricher; Docling's native PPTX backend does not expose connector source/target linkage to this mapper)

visual_fact_accuracy.matching_rule: VisualFactAnnotation recovery -- structurally not_applicable to path A (no VisionEnricher, Stage 5A never produces VisualFactAnnotation)

ocr_whole_page_text_recall.matching_rule: TEXT_NORMALIZED_V1(case_fold=True): unicode NFC -> CRLF/CR normalized to LF -> horizontal whitespace collapsed -> each line stripped -> casefold(). No punctuation stripping (punctuation is part of fact meaning in this benchmark). -- scored as text recovery, independent of OCR-origin classification (Stage 5A maps whole-page OCR as a plain paragraph, per D-035)
